task: Add rate limiting to the public API and prove it holds under load without breaking existing endpoints
loop_altitude_rationale: Multi-module change (middleware + load harness) touching a public surface, but reversible and single-agent — sequential gated stages, no parallel fan-out needed.

selection_log.why: runnable checks exist (contract suite, ratelimit test, load harness exit code) — done is machine-decidable
captured baseline is handed characterize->implement; a green 429 middleware is handed implement->load_verify; each seam is a stable checkable artifact
characterize searches the existing contract; implement is risky middleware needing mid-flight correction; load_verify is a retryable assertion
reversible dev-only change, checks discriminate well — human reviews at gates, not each iteration
stages are strictly sequential (each depends on the prior); no independent fan-out work
per-stage caps; load_verify loops back to implement, implement loops back to characterize; escalate on any public-API change
the contract/ratelimit checks are fast and cheap (npm test in seconds), so frequent small passes (implement cap 8) beat few long ones — tuned: higher caps, retry acceptable on the load stage

definition_of_done.goal: the existing public-endpoint contract suite passes and is captured as the regression set the later stages must keep green

feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: any existing endpoint returns a different status/body than the captured baseline — the suite goes red
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: a stale/empty contract suite that vacuously passes with zero assertions — ruled out: the suite asserts a concrete status+body per endpoint and fails if the captured baseline file is empty

failure: contract suite cannot be made green on the untouched baseline (environment broken, not our change)

definition_of_done.goal: rate-limit middleware returns HTTP 429 past the configured threshold and the contract regression set still passes

feedback_signal.check: npm test api:ratelimit && npm test api:contract
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: the 101st request inside the window still returns 200 (no 429), or any contract test regresses
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: middleware returns 429 for EVERY request (even below the threshold), passing a naive 'a 429 appeared' test — ruled out: the ratelimit test asserts 200 below the threshold AND 429 only above it

failure: the contract regression set goes red (behavior change leaked)

definition_of_done.goal: p99 latency stays under budget at the target RPS with zero 5xx, asserted by the load harness exit code

feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: p99 exceeds the budget or any 5xx is observed at the target RPS — the harness exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: the harness reports green because it never actually reached the target RPS (connection-capped) — ruled out: the harness asserts achieved RPS >= target before it judges p99/5xx, exiting non-zero otherwise

failure: p99 budget cannot be met without a public-API change

maker_checker.scope: fresh-context reviewer sees only the diff + the per-stage acceptance checks; flags correctness / stated-requirement gaps only, especially any behavior change to existing endpoints

harness_primitives: git worktree isolation for reversible work
external scratch ledger (.loop/run-state.md) holding which stage is done and what's next across context resets
pre-commit hook runs the current stage's check as a deterministic gate

stop_conditions.success: every stage's check passes in dependency order and the diff stays within the rate-limit middleware + load harness
failure: any stage exhausts its max_iterations without passing its check
a downstream stage forces a redesign of an already-accepted upstream stage twice
escalate: production permission or real traffic is required to verify
the load target only reproduces against prod

risk_guards.risk: reward hacking / test overfitting (agent weakens the rate-limit test to pass)
risk_guards.mitigation: the load_verify stage's check is independent of the unit tests; the checker diffs test files

risk_guards.risk: error amplification across stages (a wrong characterize baseline poisons later gates)
risk_guards.mitigation: stage gates run in dependency order; a downstream failure that implicates an upstream gate loops back rather than head-banging in place
no production resources; load test runs against an isolated dev target with least-privilege tokens
